# Setup Checklist: Exam Monitoring Dashboard

**Purpose**: Validate that Phase 1 (Setup / Shared Infrastructure) requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation/review.
**Created**: 2026-02-28
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are Phase 1 setup requirements explicitly defined for all required backend artifacts (project skeleton, settings split, ASGI, URLs, Celery bootstrap)? [Completeness, Tasks Phase 1 T001-T002] *(Addressed: tasks.md Phase 1 explicitly lists T001-T012 covering Django skeleton, settings base/dev/prod, ASGI, URLs, Celery, Docker Compose, go2rtc, .env.example, pytest, nginx.)*
- [x] CHK002 Are infrastructure requirements explicitly specified for all local dependencies (PostgreSQL, Redis, go2rtc) including required versions and roles? [Completeness, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Prerequisites] *(Addressed: Quickstart Prerequisites table: PostgreSQL 16 (primary DB), Redis 7 (channel layer/cache/Celery broker), go2rtc ≥1.9 (WebRTC relay). Plan Technical Context reiterates.)*
- [x] CHK003 Are environment variable requirements complete for runtime, storage, retention, and broker settings with clear ownership of defaults vs overrides? [Completeness, Quickstart §3.3, Gap] *(Addressed: Quickstart §3.3 + .env.example define all required env vars: DB credentials, Redis URL, secret key, debug flag, CORS origins, allowed hosts. Data-model retention table provides defaults.)*
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements documented for the expected backend folder/module boundaries and app-per-concern structure before Phase 2 starts? [Completeness, Plan §Project Structure, Tasks Phase 1] *(Addressed: Plan §Project Structure defines complete directory tree with 11 Django apps, core utilities, config split. Each app has defined responsibilities in project structure comments.)*
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for setup-time tooling baselines (linting, tests, dependency pinning) rather than leaving them as implementation assumptions? [Completeness, Tasks T003/T005, Gap] *(Addressed: Plan specifies pytest+pytest-django+pytest-cov for backend, Vitest for frontend. Spec Assumptions: pip-tools workflow (requirements.in ranges + requirements.txt pinned). Plan includes ESLint+Ruff for linting, tsc+mypy for types.)*

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "shared infrastructure" concretely scoped (what is in Phase 1 vs deferred to later phases) with no ambiguous ownership? [Clarity, Tasks Phase 1, Ambiguity] *(Addressed: tasks.md Phase 1 (T001-T012) is explicitly bounded. Phase 2 Foundation (T013-T030) is separate. Checkpoint note: "Foundation complete — all user stories can now begin implementation.")*
- [x] CHK007 Are dependency version constraints stated unambiguously (exact pins vs ranges) for reproducible setup outcomes? [Clarity, Quickstart §3.2, Tasks T003] *(Addressed: Spec Assumptions §Dependency management: "requirements.in contains compatible version ranges, compiled to fully pinned requirements.txt via pip-compile." Clarification Round Feb 28 confirms both files committed.)*
- [x] CHK008 Is "development-ready" quantified with objective setup-ready conditions (e.g., services reachable, migrations runnable, server start prerequisites)? [Clarity, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.1: Dev-ready checklist — PostgreSQL accepts connections, Redis PING returns PONG, migrations apply without errors, runserver starts, go2rtc /api/config returns 200.)*
- [x] CHK009 Are requirements clear on whether `docker-compose.dev.yml` is mandatory or optional relative to native installs? [Clarity, Quickstart §2, Ambiguity] *(Addressed: Quickstart §2 presents Docker Compose as "recommended" with explicit fallback: "If you prefer native installs, create the database manually" with SQL commands. Both paths documented.)*
- [x] CHK010 Is the expected content of `.env.example` specified sufficiently to avoid missing keys and hidden local assumptions? [Clarity, Tasks T005, Quickstart §3.3] *(Addressed: Quickstart §3.3 documents .env.example with all required keys. Implemented .env.example at repo root covers DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, REDIS_URL, SECRET_KEY, DEBUG, ALLOWED_HOSTS, CORS_ORIGINS.)*

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do setup dependency requirements remain consistent between plan technical context and quickstart dependency guidance? [Consistency, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Prerequisites] *(Addressed: Plan and Quickstart both reference same versions: Python 3.11+, Django 5.x, PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7, go2rtc ≥1.9. Consistent across all artifacts.)*
- [x] CHK012 Do setup requirements align with architecture assumptions about ASGI + Channels + Celery + Redis roles without conflicting runtime models? [Consistency, Plan §Summary/Technical Context] *(Addressed: Plan §Summary documents full stack: Django ASGI + Channels (WebSocket), Celery (async tasks), Redis (channel layer + cache + Celery broker). No conflicting models. Config/asgi.py implements ProtocolTypeRouter.)*
- [x] CHK013 Are security-related setup requirements consistent between reverse-proxy/session-cookie assumptions and local development instructions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, Quickstart §2/§3, Assumption] *(Addressed: Settings split handles this: development.py uses insecure cookies + CORS_ALLOW_ALL; production.py enforces HTTPS, secure cookies, HSTS. nginx.conf handles proxy. Consistent dev/prod separation.)*
- [x] CHK014 Are retention-related setup requirements consistent with the retention values required for audit, detections, recordings, and exports? [Consistency, Spec §FR-031, Quickstart §3.3] *(Addressed: data-model §Data Retention table defines defaults: DetectionFrame 90 days, AnomalyEvent 365 days, Recording 30 days, AuditLogEntry 365 days, SessionExport 7 days. .env.example can house these as configurable values.)*

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Are setup acceptance criteria measurable (e.g., explicit commands/outcomes) rather than descriptive only? [Acceptance Criteria, Measurability, Quickstart §2-§3, Gap] *(Addressed: Quickstart provides explicit commands: `docker compose up -d`, `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `python manage.py migrate`, `python manage.py runserver`. Verifiable outcomes: services running, migrations applied, server starts.)*
- [x] CHK016 Can Phase 1 completion be objectively evaluated independently from Phase 2 model implementation? [Acceptance Criteria, Tasks Phase 1, Gap] *(Addressed: tasks.md Phase 1 has discrete deliverables (skeleton, settings, Docker, pytest). Phase 2 starts with model creation. Phase 1 completion = server starts with empty apps, Docker services healthy, pytest runs.)*
- [x] CHK017 Are failure criteria for setup prerequisites defined (what constitutes a blocked setup state)? [Acceptance Criteria, Exception Flow, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.2: Blocked state = any of: DB unreachable, Redis unreachable, migrations fail, SECRET_KEY empty/default. setup-check.sh script validates all preconditions.)*
- [x] CHK018 Do requirements define measurable readiness for observability bootstrap needed later (health endpoint prerequisites, logging availability)? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-038/FR-039/FR-040, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.8: Observability bootstrap — JSON structured logging from Phase 1. Health endpoint in Phase 2 depends on Django management command and cache framework. Logging available immediately.)*

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are primary setup requirements documented for both Windows and Linux development environments? [Coverage, Spec §SC-009, Quickstart §Prerequisites] *(Addressed: Quickstart Prerequisites notes: "Windows: Install PostgreSQL and Redis via their official installers or use Docker." Plan Target Platform: "Windows + Linux (cross-platform)." SC-009: "operates without degradation on both Windows and Linux.")*
- [x] CHK020 Are alternate setup scenarios addressed (Docker-first vs native services) with clear requirement equivalence? [Coverage, Quickstart §2, Ambiguity] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.3: Docker and native paths documented as equivalent. Same env vars, same migration commands, same verification criteria. Docker = recommended default, native = supported alternative.)*
- [x] CHK021 Are exception scenarios defined for missing/unreachable dependencies (DB down, Redis unavailable, go2rtc offline) during setup? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.2: Setup validates all dependencies. Missing DB/Redis = clear error message with install instructions. go2rtc offline = warning only (non-blocking for backend dev).)*
- [x] CHK022 Are recovery requirements defined for interrupted setup (partial compose startup, failed migration, stale env values)? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.2: Partial compose — `docker compose down && docker compose up -d`. Failed migration — fix and re-run, Django tracks applied migrations. Stale env — restart services after .env changes.)*

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are requirements defined for port-collision cases (5432/6379/1984/8555 already in use) and required remediation behavior? [Edge Case, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.4: Ports configurable via .env (DB_PORT, REDIS_PORT, GO2RTC_PORT, DJANGO_PORT). Collision detected by setup-check script with clear error: "Port {X} already in use — set {ENV_VAR} to override.")*
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements defined for invalid or unsafe env values (empty secret key, malformed URLs, unsupported devices)? [Edge Case, Security, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.5: Django settings validate on startup — empty SECRET_KEY raises ImproperlyConfigured, malformed DB URL fails fast, invalid REDIS_URL tested on startup. Development warns; production blocks.)*
- [x] CHK025 Are requirements defined for clean initial setup when backend directory already exists partially from prior attempts? [Edge Case, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.2: General guidance — `rm -rf` and re-clone for clean state. Docker: `docker compose down -v` removes volumes. Migrations are idempotent by design.)*
- [x] CHK026 Are requirements explicit about behavior when optional GPU tooling is absent but CPU fallback is needed? [Edge Case, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Prerequisites] *(Addressed: Plan §Technical Context: "Ultralytics 8.x: Object detection inference (GPU optional, CPU fallback)." Quickstart Prerequisites: "GPU — Optional but recommended; CPU inference supported." Explicit fallback documented.)*

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK027 Are setup requirements traceably connected to latency/performance targets that depend on infrastructure choices (e.g., Redis/WS stack readiness)? [NFR, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-002, Plan §Performance Goals] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §10.6: DB query target <200ms p95 traceable to indexed fields. Redis channel layer capacity=100. WS latency depends on local network. Infrastructure choices documented with performance implications.)*
- [x] CHK028 Are security requirements for setup documented for secret handling and non-production credential constraints? [NFR, Security, Quickstart §2/§3.3, Assumption] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.6: .env.example has placeholder values, not real secrets. .gitignore includes .env. Development: django-insecure-* prefix on SECRET_KEY warns. Production: SECRET_KEY from environment, not .env file.)*
- [x] CHK029 Are observability requirements in setup specified to ensure later health dashboard metrics are attainable from initialized services? [NFR, Observability, Spec §FR-038/FR-039/FR-040] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.8: Phase 1 enables JSON logging + correlation IDs. Phase 2 health endpoint reads service status. Redis needed for health cache. PostgreSQL needed for DB health check. All initialized in Phase 1 setup.)*
- [x] CHK030 Are durability requirements during restart/failure reflected in setup requirements (service restart policy, data persistence volumes)? [NFR, Spec §Availability statement, Quickstart §2] *(Addressed: Quickstart §2 Docker Compose defines `restart: unless-stopped` for all services. PostgreSQL uses named volume for data persistence. Redis uses appendonly persistence. go2rtc mounts config volume.)*

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are external dependency assumptions (go2rtc API behavior, Redis single-node limits, PostgreSQL local auth model) explicitly documented? [Dependency, Assumption, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.7: External dependencies table — go2rtc: REST API /api/config, /api/streams; WHEP at /api/webrtc. Redis: single-node, no clustering. PostgreSQL: local md5/scram-sha-256 auth. Assumptions documented.)*
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that one `.env.example` can satisfy all local workflows validated or segmented by profile (dev/test)? [Dependency, Assumption, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.5: Single .env.example for dev. Test uses separate Django settings (settings/test.py) with in-memory overrides. No separate .env.test needed — pytest overrides via conftest.py. Environmental segmentation deferred to v2.)*
- [x] CHK033 Are required ordering dependencies for setup steps clearly specified (infra up → env config → install deps → migrate → run services)? [Dependency, Quickstart §2-§3] *(Addressed: Quickstart §2-§3 follows explicit ordering: 1) Clone repo, 2) Start infrastructure (Docker/native), 3) Copy .env.example → .env, 4) Create virtualenv + install deps, 5) Run migrations, 6) Create superuser, 7) Start dev server. Step-by-step numbering enforces order.)*
- [x] CHK034 Are assumptions about frontend/backend startup coupling during Phase 1 explicitly documented (or intentionally excluded)? [Dependency, Boundary, Gap] *(Addressed: Plan §Summary: frontend is Phase 3, backend is Phases 1-2. tasks-frontend.md is a separate task file. Quickstart: backend and frontend are separate sections. No coupling assumed in Phase 1.)*

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK035 Is there any unresolved conflict between "pinned dependencies" in tasks and version-range examples in quickstart requirements? [Conflict, Tasks T003, Quickstart §3.2] *(Addressed: Spec Assumptions §Dependency management: "requirements.in contains compatible version ranges, compiled to fully pinned requirements.txt via pip-compile." Both files committed. No conflict — ranges in .in, pins in .txt. Clarification Round Feb 28 confirms workflow.)*
- [x] CHK036 Are terms like "recommended," "optional," and "development-ready" defined with enforceable criteria to avoid interpretation drift in reviews? [Ambiguity, Gap] *(Resolved in spec-addendum §14.1: "Development-ready" defined with 5 objective conditions. "Recommended" = Docker path. "Optional" = GPU, go2rtc for frontend-only dev. Enforceable via setup-check script.)*

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
